denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 03/10/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W101314554          Group #: GRP-1166
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-126763
Claim #: CLM-2026-196799
Date of Service (requested): 02/18/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 02/20/2026 for GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg) related to Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg)
Diagnosis (as submitted): Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 106 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 106 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg) is not considered evidence-based for Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01) under current medical policy. Published criteria do not support authorization.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination before or during the appeal process., they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W101314554) and reference UM-2026-126763. 
Funding: This determination applies under your employer's self-funded group arrangement (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. They has been treated under commercial coverage with GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg) recommended as the next appropriate step. BMI 42; HbA1c 6.2%; failed structured lifestyle program 6 months. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that not evidence based bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if their care is delayed further.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: The brief matches the assigned Cigna / endocrine cell and executes sub_tactic 'not_evidence_based' with a clinically appropriate Obesity with BMI 42 and prediabetes (E66.01) → GLP-1 receptor agonist (semaglutide 2.4 mg) pair.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Cigna correspondence with MCG Care Guidelines and procedural tone.
